label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking if following these steps will bring me back to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward, take 2 steps backward, take 5 steps backward, then 5 steps right, then 9 steps right. The options are Yes or No. Hmm, let me break it down. First, I need to visualize this. If I start at a point, facing forward. Then the first action is taking 2 steps backward. So, if I'm facing forward, moving backward would be in the opposite direction of where I'm facing. So, my position after the first two steps would be 2 steps behind my starting point. Then, the next instruction is take 5 steps backward. Again, since I'm still facing the same direction (forward), moving backward another 5 steps. So total steps backward would be 2 + 5 = 7 steps behind the starting point. Now the direction changes. The next instruction is take 5 steps right. Since I'm facing forward, right would be to my right. So, if I imagine a coordinate system where forward is the positive y-direction, then right would be the positive x-direction. So, moving 5 steps to the right from where I am now, which is 7 steps behind the starting point. So I move 5 steps in the positive x-direction (right) and remain at -7 in the y-direction (since I'm 7 steps behind). Then, the final instruction is take 9 steps right. Again, to the right, which is the same x-direction. So adding another 9 steps to the right. So total steps to the right is 5 + 9 = 14 steps. So my final position is 14 steps to the right (positive x-direction) and 7 steps behind (negative y-direction) from the starting point. So, to check if I'm back to the starting point, I need to have both x and y coordinates as zero. But in this case, my x is 14 and y is -7. So, not zero. Therefore, I haven't returned to the starting point. So the answer should be No. Wait, but maybe I misread the instructions. Let me double-check. The instructions say: always face forward. Take 2 steps backward, then 5 steps backward. Then 5 steps right, then 9 steps right.
